_note: EXAMPLE DATA -- not real search results. Demonstrates the literature_map.schema.json format with all fields populated.
topic: Multi-agent LLM orchestration for complex task decomposition

papers.title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
papers.abstract: AutoGen is an open-source framework that allows developers to build LLM applications via multiple agents that can converse with each other to solve tasks.
papers.contribution: Introduces a multi-agent conversation framework where LLM agents collaborate through structured dialogue, supporting customizable agent roles and human-in-the-loop patterns.
papers.limitation: Focuses on conversation-based coordination; does not address fine-grained task decomposition or dynamic agent spawning based on subtask complexity.

papers.title: AgentVerse: Facilitating Multi-Agent Collaboration and Exploring Emergent Behaviors
papers.abstract: AgentVerse provides a framework for multi-agent collaboration that supports dynamic agent group adjustment and explores emergent collaborative behaviors.
papers.contribution: Demonstrates that dynamically adjusting the composition of agent groups during task solving leads to better performance than static agent assignments, with emergent specialization patterns.
papers.limitation: Agent group dynamics are rule-based rather than learned; scaling beyond 10 agents shows diminishing returns without clear mitigation.

papers.title: Language Agent Tree Search Unifies Reasoning Acting and Planning in Language Models
papers.abstract: LATS introduces a general framework that synergizes the capabilities of LLMs for reasoning, acting, and planning by leveraging Monte Carlo Tree Search.
papers.contribution: Unifies reasoning (Chain-of-Thought), acting (ReAct), and planning (Tree-of-Thoughts) into a single MCTS-based framework, achieving state-of-the-art on HumanEval and WebShop benchmarks.
papers.limitation: Computational cost is 3-5x higher than single-pass methods due to tree expansion; does not explore multi-agent variants where different agents handle different tree branches.

clusters.name: multi-agent-frameworks
clusters.description: Systems for orchestrating multiple LLM agents in collaborative or competitive configurations

clusters.name: reasoning-planning
clusters.description: Methods for structured reasoning and planning in LLM-based agents

exclusion_reasons: Not peer-reviewed and low citation count
